channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Memory Price Surge Forces Smartphone Makers to Downgrade Entry-Level Specs
item.description: Surging DRAM and NAND prices push smartphone makers to revert entry-level models from 12GB to 6GB RAM, as Xiaomi's Lei Jun warns the price rally may last two more years.

item.title: DiDi Rebrands: Drops 'Chuxing' From App Name as Platform Strategy Expands
item.description: China's largest ride-hailing platform DiDi removes 'Chuxing' from its brand, launches App 8.0 with four-pillar structure, and integrates with WeChat's AI ecosystem.

item.title: UBTECH's U1 Humanoid Companion Robot Nears 4,000 Pre-Orders in 10 Days
item.description: UBTECH's full-size U1 companion humanoid robot garners nearly 4,000 pre-orders with over 10 million yuan in deposits within 10 days of launch.

item.title: Zhipu AI Open-Sources GLM-5.2 With 1 Million Token Context
item.description: Zhipu AI announces GLM-5.2 will be released under MIT license with 1M token context as a direct response to US export restrictions targeting Anthropic models.

item.title: Intermittent Slow Cache Responses
item.description: Jun 14, 03:40 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 14, 03:30 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 13, 23:43 UTC Identified - The cause of this issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 13, 23:22 UTC Investigating - We...

item.title: WeChat Is Building an AI Agent Too — But Whose Interests Will It Really Serve?
item.description: As Tencent tests WeChat's built-in AI Agent, industry analysts debate whether it represents genuine innovation or platform defense — and whether true user-side AI Agents offer a more transformative vision.

item.title: NPM 12 Will Change Script Execution Behavior to Prevent Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: By default, npm install will no longer execute scripts from dependencies, unless explicitly allowed. The post NPM 12 Will Change Script Execution Behavior to Prevent Supply Chain Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Critical Splunk Enterprise Flaw Lets Attackers Run Code Without Authentication
item.description: Splunk has released security updates to address a critical security flaw in Splunk Enterprise that could be exploited to conduct unauthenticated file operations and even remote code execution. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20253, is rated 9.8 on the CVSS scoring system. "In Splunk...

item.title: Anthropic Says It Has Taken Its Latest AI Models Offline to Comply With New Export Controls
item.description: Anthropic takes Fable 5 and Mythos 5 offline to comply with a directive from the Trump administration to prevent use by foreign nationals. The post Anthropic Says It Has Taken Its Latest AI Models Offline to Comply With New Export Controls appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals
item.description: Anthropic said on Friday it will "abruptly disable" its most advanced artificial intelligence (AI) models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, for all users after the U.S. government ordered it to suspend access to the models for foreign nationals, whether inside or outside the U.S., citing national...

item.title: [AINews] Fable and Mythos officially too dangerous to release
item.description: We are in the strangest timeline.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.8
item.description: Jun 13, 02:11 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 13, 02:03 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: We’ve suspended access to Claude Mythos 5 and Claude Fable 5
item.description: Jun 13, 00:50 UTC Monitoring - Learn more here: https://anthropic.com/news/fable-mythos-access

item.title: INC20000033
item.description: Jun 12, 20:57 UTC Resolved - Current status: We've implemented the fix for this issue and monitored the environment to confirm that service was restored. If you experience additional issues or have questions, please open a support case via Snowflake Community or the Support page in...

item.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
item.description: Attackers took over more than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) this week and rewrote their build scripts to install a credential stealer on any machine that built them. The malware is a Rust binary built to harvest developer secrets. When it lands with root, it can also load an eBPF...

item.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Accused of Using Gemini AI in Phishing
item.description: Google on Friday said it's pursuing legal action against a Chinese cybercrime network, accusing it of using its Gemini artificial intelligence (AI) agent to send phishing text messages targeting Americans. The network is said to be behind the development and management of a phishing-as-a-service...

item.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade
item.description: Instead of hiding on the laptops and servers defenders watch most closely, a China-nexus group spent close to a decade hidden inside the Linux login system itself. Sygnia, which tracks the group as Velvet Ant, says it backdoored the PAM and OpenSSH components that decide who is allowed to sign in,...

item.title: In Other News: Google Security Layoffs, AudiA6 Takedown, $400 Million Coupang Fine
item.description: Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: ICS device exposure remains flat as attack surface widens, Microsoft issues incident response playbook for AI, IBM and AT&T accused of hack cover-ups. The post In Other News: Google Security Layoffs, AudiA6 Takedown, $400...

item.title: olmo-eval: An evaluation workbench for the model development loop
Cloudflare Dashboard and Cloudflare API service issues
item.description: Jun 12, 15:03 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 12, 14:56 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 12, 14:27 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues with Cloudflare Dashboard and related APIs....

item.title: Industry Reactions to Claude Fable 5: Feedback Friday
item.description: Industry professionals comment on various aspects of Fable 5, including dual-use capabilities, safeguards, and tiered access. The post Industry Reactions to Claude Fable 5: Feedback Friday appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: “reprogramming” aging, and the hidden sense of interoception
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Why “reprogramming” is the buzziest approach to reversing aging right now Earlier this week, Life Biosciences, a biotech company focused on reversing...

item.title: Iranian Cyber Group Handala Claims Cal Water Hack
item.description: The hackers published 5GB of data, including customer personal information and credentials for the RTKBase platform. The post Iranian Cyber Group Handala Claims Cal Water Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Waiting Room analytics showing a drop in number of active users
item.description: Jun 12, 11:14 UTC Update - The issue has been identified, and a fix is being implemented. Jun 12, 10:55 UTC Identified - We are currently investigating an issue where Waiting Room analytics are showing a drop in the number of active users.

item.title: You do your own time
item.description: There we were, a regular murderers’ row of librarians. Little Jo. Eustace. And me. Turning around in the nave of our library to greet the sound of footsteps, pistols leveled in case whoever was coming in didn’t respect sanctuary. Little Jo had a stack of books under one arm. Eustace was holding the...

item.title: Turnstile loading issues
item.description: Jun 12, 09:30 UTC Resolved - During the timeframe between 09:46 to 16:27UTC today on June 12th users may have observed Turnstile not loading or solving the challenges. This is now resolved.

item.title: Why “reprogramming” is the buzziest approach to reversing aging right now
item.description: Earlier this week, Life Biosciences, a biotech company focused on reversing age-related diseases, announced that it had dosed its first volunteer. A person with glaucoma has had an experimental treatment injected straight into their eyeball. The idea is to try to treat the disease—which can cause...

item.title: Inside Interoception: The hidden sense of how you feel inside
item.description: MIT Technology Review Explains: Let our writers untangle the complex, messy world of science and technology to help you understand what’s coming next. You can read more from the series here. Your brain lives in the dark space of your skull. Yet it knows when the wind lifts the hairs on...

item.title: [AINews] Loopcraft: The Art of Stacking Loops
item.description: a quiet day lets us highlight a great concept from Peter Steinberger, Boris Cherny, and Andrej Karpathy

item.title: ToolSense: A Diagnostic Framework for Auditing Parametric Tool Knowledge in LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2606.12451v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models deployed as agents over large tool catalogs face a critical tool-retrieval bottleneck. As embedding-based retrieval approaches rely on compact encoders that may under-capture specialized tool semantics, parametric tool retrieval...

item.title: Arbor: Tree Search as a Cognition Layer for Autonomous Agents
item.description: arXiv:2606.12563v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Arbor is a multi-agent framework that introduces structured tree search as a cognition layer for autonomous agents operating in large, stateful action spaces. Prior autonomous optimization systems operate on isolated targets with stateless evaluation....

item.title: Strategic Decision Support for AI Agents
item.description: arXiv:2606.12587v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Traditionally, decision support studies how humans use machine learning models to make better decisions. In modern agentic systems, this division of roles is increasingly reversed: AI agents act on behalf of users, while humans and tools becomes...

item.title: Pythagoras-Prover: Advancing Efficient Formal Proving via Augmented Lean Formalisation
item.description: arXiv:2606.12594v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern Lean theorem provers achieve strong performance only with substantial training and inference compute, driven in part by scarce verified proof data and the long reasoning traces of formal proof search, making both supervised fine-tuning (SFT)...

item.title: PersonaDrive: Human-Style Retrieval-Augmented VLA Agents for Closed-Loop Driving Simulation
item.description: arXiv:2606.12616v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Closed-loop driving simulators typically populate their environments with non-ego traffic agents that behave largely the same way, produced either by rule-based traffic managers or by learned models trained toward a single behavioral mode. Recent work...

item.title: Physics-informed generative AI for semiconductor manufacturing: Enforcing hard physical constraints in generative models by construction
item.description: arXiv:2606.11247v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generative models are increasingly used to propose designs, data, and control actions for physical systems, yet many such systems are governed by hard physical constraints rather than by perceptual plausibility. Semiconductor manufacturing provides a...

item.title: Mechanical Field Networks: Structured Neural Dynamics for Multivariate Systems
item.description: arXiv:2606.11251v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Many multivariate dynamical systems are observed only through trajectories, leaving the mechanisms governing their joint dynamics hidden. Existing approaches can impose interpretable dynamics or learn flexible state transitions, yet the resulting...

item.title: Bernstein-Schur Kernels: Random Features by Sketched Modulation and Radial Randomization
item.description: arXiv:2606.11255v2 Announce Type: new Abstract: Bernstein--Schur kernels are products of a finite-feature kernel and a completely monotone shift-invariant kernel: nonstationary kernels falling between the shift-invariant and dot-product templates random features exploit, so neither Bochner sampling...

item.title: Loss Landscape Diagnosis for Gradient-Based Gray-Scott System Inversion: Disentangling the Roles of PINN Components
item.description: arXiv:2606.11258v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Gradient-based inversion of reaction-diffusion systems is typically approached via surrogate models or physics-informed neural networks (PINNs), while the most direct route, backpropagation through the PDE's structure itself, has largely been avoided....

item.title: PermDoRA -- Understanding Adapter Interference in Language Models: Limits of Parameter-Space Geometry
item.description: arXiv:2606.11262v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Access control in large language models (LLMs) requires modular mechanisms to enable domain-specific behavior without retraining or cross-domain interference. A common hypothesis is that interference during adapter composition arises from overlap in...

item.title: EDEN: A Large-Scale Corpus of Clinical Notes for Italian
item.description: arXiv:2606.12569v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present EDEN (Emergency Department Electronic Notes), a new and unique large-scale corpus of clinical notes produced in Emergency Departments of Italian hospitals. The corpus, in its current version, is composed of approximately 4 million clinical...

item.title: Helping Figures Tell their Story! Paper-Grounded Video Generation Explaining Complex Scientific Figures
item.description: arXiv:2606.12576v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Scientific figures compress complex pipelines into a single canvas, yet understanding them requires paper-grounded, step-by-step narration aligned with visual highlights a capability missing from current video generation systems and benchmarks. To...

item.title: MARD: Mirror-Augmented Reasoning Distillation for Mechanism-Level Drug-Drug Interaction Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2606.12578v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mechanism-level drug-drug interaction (DDI) prediction requires identifying which enzyme or pharmacodynamic axis is implicated, in which direction, and with which evidence -- not merely whether two drugs interact. We introduce a reproducible...

item.title: Constrained Semantic Decompression in LLMs through Persian Proverb-Conditioned Story Generation
item.description: arXiv:2606.12599v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Transforming a dense, abstract proverb into an engaging and morally faithful narrative requires deep cultural understanding and robust semantic grounding. We frame this problem as a \emph{constrained semantic decompression} task and study...

item.title: Shopping Reasoning Bench: An Expert-Authored Benchmark for Multi-Turn Conversational Shopping Assistants
item.description: arXiv:2606.12608v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Conversational shopping assistants now serve hundreds of millions of customers, yet no existing benchmark jointly evaluates the open-ended multi-turn reasoning, domain expertise, and criterion-level quality that real shopping conversations demand....

item.title: Network Performance Issues in Brisbane, QLD, Australia - (BNE)
item.description: Jun 12, 01:39 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 12, 01:30 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 12, 01:24 UTC Update - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue. Jun 12, 01:17 UTC Identified - Cloudflare is investigating issues...

item.title: Elevated 431 Errors
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Sora (Operational) Images (Operational) Chat Completions (Operational) Fine-tuning (Operational) Files (Operational) Moderations (Operational) Responses (Operational) Login (Operational) Batch (Operational) Audio...

item.title: Elevated error rates for GPT 5.5 in Codex
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components Codex Web (Degraded performance) CLI (Degraded performance) App (Degraded performance) VS Code extension (Degraded performance) Codex API (Degraded performance)

item.title: Incident with Webhooks
item.description: Jun 11, 20:00 UTC Monitoring - The degradation affecting Webhooks has been mitigated. We are monitoring to ensure stability. Jun 11, 19:58 UTC Update - We have applied a mitigation and are monitoring for recovery. Jun 11, 19:42 UTC Update - We are currently experiencing delays in Web Hook delivery...

item.title: Understanding Truncated Positional Encodings for Graph Neural Networks
item.description: Positional encodings (PEs) enhance the power of graph neural networks (GNNs), both theoretically and empirically. Two of the most popular families of PEs - spectral (e.g., Laplacian eigenspaces, effective resistance) and walk-based (polynomials of the adjacency matrix) - are theoretically...

item.title: Elevated errors across models
item.description: Jun 11, 17:56 UTC Resolved - The issue affecting various models has been resolved. Impact occurred from 09:42am PT / 16:42 UTC to 10:03am PT / 17:03 UTC. Jun 11, 17:27 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 11, 16:54 UTC Investigating - We are currently...
